assertions.title: pyproject.toml is the primary build configuration

steps.observed: [build-system] at line 1, [project] at line 4, [project.optional-dependencies] sections present (dev, doc, server)

steps.action: Check setup.cfg has no [metadata] or [options] sections
steps.observed: setup.cfg contains only [versioneer] section — no [metadata] or [options] sections

steps.action: Check setup.py is minimal with only custom commands
steps.expected: setup.py only has custom Command classes and setup(cmdclass=...)
steps.observed: setup.py imports setuptools only, defines DevelopCommand, InstallCommand, NPMBuildCommand, BuildPyCommand, and calls setup(cmdclass={...}) with no metadata

evidence.setup_cfg: Only contains [versioneer] config section — no metadata or options
evidence.setup_py: Imports only from setuptools, defines 4 custom command classes, setup() has only cmdclass argument

steps.observed: ls /Users/dwasserm/sources/NeuroLang/versioneer.py → No such file or directory
ls /Users/dwasserm/sources/NeuroLang/neurolang/_version.py → No such file or directory

steps.action: Check MANIFEST.in does not reference versioneer
steps.observed: MANIFEST.in contains only 'include pyproject.toml' — no versioneer references

steps.expected: importlib.metadata.version() usage instead of versioneer get_versions()
steps.observed: Uses 'from importlib.metadata import version, PackageNotFoundError' and '__version__ = version("neurolang")'

evidence.versioneer_py: File absent (ls returns no such file)
evidence._version_py: File absent (ls returns no such file)

steps.expected: All install_requires present; extras (dev, doc, server) present; no overly restrictive upper bounds like lark<1.1.3
steps.observed: dependencies list includes numpy, scipy, nibabel>=5.0.0, nilearn>=0.10.0, pandas>=1.5, pysdd, lark>=1.1.2 (lower bound only, no upper bound), scikit-learn, matplotlib, seaborn, typing_inspect, rdflib>=6.0.0, sqlalchemy>=1.4. Optional deps: dev (pytest>=7, pytest-benchmark, pytest-cov, tornado, pyyaml), doc (sphinx>=4.2, etc.), server (tornado, pyyaml)

evidence.dependencies: 13 packages in [project] dependencies, all with lower-bound-only constraints or no bounds. lark uses '>=1.1.2' (no upper bound). Three optional-dependency groups: dev, doc, server.
evidence.no_setup_cfg_deps: setup.cfg has no [options] or install_requires — all deps are in pyproject.toml

steps.action: Check [project.scripts] in pyproject.toml for neuro-server entry point
Check [tool.setuptools.package-data] in pyproject.toml for neurolang-web and datalog patterns

evidence.package_data: pyproject.toml lines 76-83 show [tool.setuptools.package-data] with neurolang key containing all required patterns including neurolang-web/dist/* and frontend/datalog/*.json

steps.observed: grep shows: [tool.pytest.ini_options] at line 88, [tool.black] at line 104, [tool.isort] at line 107
grep returns no output (exit code 1 = no matches)

evidence.distutils_grep: grep -n 'distutils' setup.py → no matches. setup.py imports only: configparser, logging, shutil, subprocess, setuptools (setup, Command), setuptools.command.install (install), setuptools.command.develop (develop), setuptools.command.build_py (build_py), pathlib (Path)

VAL-BUILD-001.notes: pyproject.toml has [build-system], [project], [project.optional-dependencies]. setup.cfg has only [versioneer] section (no [metadata] or [options]). setup.py is minimal with only custom command classes.

VAL-BUILD-002.notes: versioneer.py absent. neurolang/_version.py absent. MANIFEST.in has no versioneer references (only 'include pyproject.toml'). pyproject.toml has [tool.setuptools_scm] with version_file. neurolang/__init__.py uses importlib.metadata.version().

VAL-BUILD-003.notes: All dependencies in pyproject.toml [project] dependencies and [project.optional-dependencies]. lark uses '>=1.1.2' (lower bound only, no restrictive upper bound like <1.1.3). setup.cfg has no dependency sections.

VAL-BUILD-008.notes: No distutils imports in setup.py. All commands use setuptools equivalents (install, develop, build_py from setuptools.command.*).

summary: Tested 8 assertions: all 8 passed. pyproject.toml is fully migrated with all required sections, versioneer is fully removed and replaced with setuptools-scm, all dependencies migrated with no restrictive upper bounds, entry points and package data preserved, Python >=3.12 requirement set, tool config consolidated, and distutils fully removed.
